channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Mozambique carried out a brutal crackdown on post-election protests, Amnesty says
item.description: A leading international rights group has accused Mozambique's security forces of a brutal, three-month crackdown on protests after the country's election last year.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray And Charles Mangwiro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Africa’s new US envoy called Trump racist, homophobic in 2020
item.description: South Africa’s newly appointed U.S. envoy Mcebisi Jonas has already come under fire for remarks made about President Trump as the relationship between the two countries has crumbled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An international conference rallies aid for Sudan after 2 years of civil war, but peace is elusive
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain and the European Union pledged hundreds of millions of dollars Tuesday to ease suffering in Sudan, on the second anniversary of a civil war that has killed tens of thousands of people, displaced 14 million and pushed large parts of the country into famine.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France expels 12 Algerian officials in tit-for-tat move amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France said Tuesday it was expelling 12 Algerian diplomatic officials a day after Algeria announced the expulsion of the same number of French officials in escalating tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US blasts UN for extending human rights envoy to Palestinian territories
item.description: The United States is rebuking a United Nations decision to extend Francesca Albanese’s tenure as the U.N. special rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Hamas rejects Israeli ceasefire, hostage return deal over disarmament demands
item.description: Hamas has reportedly rejected Israel's latest ceasefire offer, which included the return of humanitarian aid in exchange for nearly a dozen hostages and Hamas' complete disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia sentences 4 journalists tied to Putin rival Navalny to nearly 6 years in prison
item.description: Four journalists were convicted of extremism on Tuesday by a Russian court and sentenced to 5 and a half years in prison for their ties to the country’s deceased opposition leader Alexei Navalny.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: People detained in Myanmar after release from scam compounds attempt an escape
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They walked out of the compound in Myanmar not knowing where they would go. Though they were aiming for the river that separated them from Thailand and freedom, they didn't know if they would make it across.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: US flies long-range bomber for drill with South Korea, as the North marks a key state anniversary
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The United States flew long-range B-1B bombers in a show of force against North Korea on Tuesday, days after the powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to resist a U.S.-led push to eliminate the North’s nuclear program. North Korea often reacts to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Another US aircraft carrier in Mideast waters ahead of second round of Iran-US nuclear talks
item.description: A second U.S. aircraft carrier is operating in Mideast waters ahead of the next round of talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia claims its deadly attack on Ukraine's Sumy targeted military forces as condemnation grows
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Russia on Monday claimed its deadly missile attack on Ukraine's Sumy that killed and wounded scores including children had targeted a gathering of Ukrainian troops, while European leaders condemned the attack as a war crime.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Attack by gunmen leaves at least 40 people dead in Nigeria, the country's president says
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — Nigeria's president said on Monday that at least 40 people were killed when Muslim gunmen, believed to be herders, attacked a Christian farming community in the north-central part of the country, the latest in an increasing wave of violence in the West African country.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump trade war with China hurting US farmers, advocate says
item.description: John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation family farmer who works 1,300 acres in Virginia, said he hopes the Trump administration will listen to farmers moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Ecuador re-elects President Daniel Noboa amid nationwide unease over crime
item.description: Conservative Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa, a wealthy anti-crime crusader, was re-elected on Sunday, defeating left-wing lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis makes appearance after Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still recovering from double pneumonia, but the 88-year-old still met with faithful after the Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican on April 13, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gazans speak out against Hamas for the first time in 18 years
item.description: Gazan residents are beginning to speak out against Hamas for the first time in 18 years after facing abject desperation in the Gaza strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9
